from_session: MPI-306 Phase 1 — the portable carousel frame, built and user-verified

session.name: App carousel frame — PHASE 1 SHIPPED; Phase 2 (Head Swap proof) is next

goal.original: Design the portable app UI frame (MPI-306), then build it.
goal.summary: Phase 1 is BUILT, self-verified, and confirmed by the user in the running app ('ok, looking good'). MpiBaseApp is now the step carousel; steps are data; the box gizmo exists. Two commits: 12a06b51 (the frame) + 9e6c4cae (a 0px-stage fix found by the user). Next session runs PHASE 2 — Head Swap as the frame's proof — then Phase 3.

completed: Mock-up phase: three mock-ups, C2 approved as THE design.

PHASE 1 (steps 1-6): frame shell + navigating ticker + inset divider + slot rendering + STEP_KINDS registry + MpiStepBox + declared `fields` + `steps` on AppDef + CSS/props registration. Committed 12a06b51.
Phase 1 follow-up fix: the carousel stage collapsed to 0px inside MpiOverlay (topbar rendered, nothing else). Committed 9e6c4cae.
Verification harnesses run against the LIVE app: 22/22 frame checks, 10/10 box checks, 12/12 geometry checks. All temp scaffolding reverted.
pending: PHASE 2 — Head Swap as the frame's proof (plan step 7). THIS IS THE NEXT ACTION.
Phase 3 — hold-until-Apply run-path change (plan step 8), deliberately a separate diff.
User: head-swap LoRA fp32-vs-fp16 A/B, then R2 upload (NOT authorised). Head Swap cannot RUN until this lands — the graph 404s on the LoRA.
User: supply the real step-0 example image/video for Head Swap (currently the placeholder `sdxl-real-05.webp`).

completed: docs/playbooks/add-app/ui/carousel-frame.md — updated with the Discard removal (both the run-path section and the approved-composition list).

docs/playbooks/add-app/existing-apps/head-swap.md — REFRESHED: the stale 'NOT YET BUILT' banner and the dead MPI-304 blocker are gone (requiredDeps shipped; Head Swap is its first consumer); the coord-convention and requiredModels open questions are marked settled.
.agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-306/validation.md — full Phase 1 evidence, the three bugs found during verification, and an explicit list of what is NOT yet validated.
.agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-306/plan.md — Phase 1 marked shipped with its two deltas.
pending: A memory entry for the mpi_box contract once the gizmo ships end-to-end (deferred three times now; still deferred until Phase 2 proves it against a real workflow).

UNCARDED CLEANUP: `--lib-bg: oklch(0.26 0.020 350)` is declared LOCALLY in MpiAppLibrary.css:34, MpiModelManager.css:39 and now MpiBaseApp.css. DELIBERATE (see carousel-frame.md), but worth a card if a fourth surface wants it.

UNCARDED: MpiVideoViewer.enterCropMode(initialRect) was silently discarding its rect — the cropTool root fix now makes that parameter work. Nobody has re-tested video crop restore; worth a look if it ever seemed off.

next_action.description: Implement PHASE 2 of .agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-306/plan.md — Head Swap as the frame's proof. Read docs/playbooks/add-app/ui/carousel-frame.md § The approved composition and § Head Swap's instance first; the design is settled and must NOT be re-derived. The work is DATA plus one small controls component: (1) declare `steps` on the head-swap AppDef — two `box` steps, role image1 ('Mark where the new head goes') and role image2 ('Mark which head to take'), each ratio 1, with tickerLabel 'Target head' / 'Reference head'; (2) declare `labels: ['Original', 'Face Reference']` on its media group so the slots stop reading 'Image 1 / Image 2'; (3) build the tier radio as Head Swap's `uiComponent` (Quality/Turbo/Hyper) using the MEASURED RELATIVE labels baseline / ~25% / ~13% — never absolute seconds — and register it in shell.js's _appComponents map; (4) make the box values reach Input_Box / Input_Box_2 as top-left source pixels via the op's injection params. THE ACCEPTANCE TEST FOR THE FRAME: if any of this needs a frame change, the frame was wrong — fix the frame, not the app. Head Swap CANNOT RUN (its LoRA 404s pending an unauthorised R2 upload), so verify by inspection + the box values landing in state, NOT by generating.

key_decisions: DISCARD IS GONE, and closing no longer prompts (user-decided this session, superseding the mock-up). Discard was redundant: 'Generate again' already overwrites a pending result and closing already drops it, so its only unique act was 'clear the pane and stay here'. With nothing unique to destroy, a close prompt guarded a non-decision. The 'Not saved yet' note SURVIVES — it is what makes Apply legible.

THE APPLY BUTTON IS HIDDEN UNTIL A RESULT EXISTS (`_applyRow.hidden = !_hasPending`). The user noticed it 'missing' — it is by design, but since Head Swap cannot run, it is never seen. OPEN QUESTION FOR PHASE 3: should Apply render DISABLED instead, so the commit step is visible before a run? Decide deliberately; do not just leave it hidden by accident.

Slot labels are APP DATA, not frame-invented. A media group may declare `labels: []` (index-aligned with roles); the frame falls back to a descriptive role, then a numbered noun. 'Image 1 / Image 2' is the fallback, not the design.
The step ticker NAVIGATES (overturns the older 'dots do not navigate'). Shipped and confirmed.
Middle steps are the bounded centred canvas — title above, optional ONE-row fields, hint below. No annotation column, no divider.

MpiOverlay is built for centred CARD content and gives its container no height + a 600px cap. Any full-bleed consumer must escape it with a `:has()` override from its OWN CSS (MpiAppLibrary and MpiModelManager already do; MpiBaseApp now does too). Never edit the shared MpiOverlay.css.

Tier cost labels are RELATIVE percentages (baseline / ~25% / ~13%), never absolute seconds — a hardcoded ETA is a lie on every GPU but the one it was measured on. Progress during a run comes from SERVER TRUTH (steps completed).
constraints: `steps` NOW EXISTS in code (introduced this session): AppDef gained `steps`, plus AppStep and AppStepField typedefs in js/data/appsRegistry.js. Phase 2 DECLARES data against it — it does not build the mechanism.

A step kind's contract is `{ media, value, onChange, step }` + `el.getValue()`. It must never learn its host app, the workflow, or any injector. STEP_KINDS lives in js/components/Organisms/MpiBaseApp/stepKinds.js — a new gizmo is one component + one line there.
MpiStepBox reports `{ box: {x,y,w,h} }` in ABSOLUTE TOP-LEFT SOURCE PIXELS, already clamped to the image. `Mpi Box` consumes that unconverted — do NOT add conversion at the injection site.
The box MUST stay clamped: `Mpi Box Crop` returns the intersection and does not pad, so an off-edge box silently yields a non-square crop — a distorted head the user blames on the model.
FIXED-PROMPT app: the graph has NO Input_Positive/Input_Negative — both prompts are BAKED. Never build a prompt box for Head Swap.
NO SEED UI, EVER — random seed every generation, never shown or saved.

The RUN PATH is untouched and stays untouched in Phase 2: submitAppGeneration still commits at ENQUEUE time (scope:'gallery' + placeholderGroup, js/services/appService.js:105-107). Apply is INERT. That is Phase 3.

Standing UI law: ComponentFactory.create (never modify js/components/factory.js), BEM, qs/qsa/gid + on/off from js/utils/dom.js, CSS vars from styles/01_base.css, icons from js/utils/icons.js, Events.on/emit with stored unsubs, a destroy() that cleans everything, register new CSS in js/shell/preloadStyles.js and document props in js/components/types.js.

MpiButton has NO setText — its label is a `.mpi-btn__text` span. There are no 'chevron-left'/'chevron-right' icons in js/utils/icons.js. Cancel is `activeGenerations.cancel(id)`, not an event. All three were discovered the hard way this session.
State: never mutate sub-objects — replace the top-level key. App inputs live in state.s_appInputs[appId].

Shared tree: commit by explicit pathspec, never `git add -A`. A concurrent session owns MPI-288/MPI-305 — do not touch them. `.agents/mpi-kanban/events.jsonl` has 3 PRE-EXISTING malformed lines (1260/1265/1324) from other sessions; leave them alone.
DO NOT upload anything to R2 — still not authorised, pending the fp32-vs-fp16 A/B.
Head Swap CANNOT RUN regardless of this work (LoRA 404). Do not treat a failed generation as a frame or app bug.

The user's app instance owns port 3000. Do NOT spawn a second one (zombie instances serve stale routes). Drive the RUNNING app; browser dev mode needs a project opened first (the app shell is display:none on the landing page).
VERIFY GEOMETRY, NOT JUST DOM PRESENCE. The first Phase 1 suite passed 22/22 while the whole stage was 0px tall. Assert real widths/heights.

rules_active: feedback: no seed UI; no toast on user Stop; error dialog vs toast

recent_events.event: user found the frame blank; 0px-stage overlay bug fixed (commit 9e6c4cae)
user confirmed the frame in the app: 'ok, looking good'

resume_prompt: Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/82fdd422-6871-4f14-9147-c7015224ac23.json, then read docs/playbooks/add-app/ui/carousel-frame.md (§ The approved composition and § Head Swap's instance) and .agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-306/plan.md. MPI-306 Phase 1 is DONE and user-verified: MpiBaseApp is now the portable step carousel, `steps` exists on AppDef, STEP_KINDS + MpiStepBox are live, and two commits are in (12a06b51, 9e6c4cae). The design is SETTLED — do not re-derive or re-litigate it. Build PHASE 2 ONLY: Head Swap as the frame's proof. That is mostly DATA — declare two `box` steps on the head-swap AppDef (roles image1 and image2, ratio 1, ticker labels 'Target head' and 'Reference head'), declare `labels: ['Original','Face Reference']` on its media group, build the Quality/Turbo/Hyper tier radio as its `uiComponent` and register it in shell.js's _appComponents map, and make the box values reach Input_Box / Input_Box_2 as top-left source pixels. The acceptance test for the frame is that NONE of this should need a frame change — if it does, fix the frame, not the app. Tier labels are the MEASURED relative percentages (baseline / ~25% / ~13%), never absolute seconds, and there is no prompt box and no seed UI. Do NOT touch the run path: that is Phase 3, so Apply stays inert. Head Swap CANNOT RUN yet (its LoRA 404s pending an unauthorised R2 upload) — verify by inspection and by the box values landing in state, never by generating, and do not treat a failed generation as a bug. Also decide deliberately in Phase 3 whether Apply should render DISABLED rather than hidden before a result exists — the user noticed its absence. The user's app owns port 3000; drive the running instance, never spawn a second one, and assert real geometry (widths/heights), because a DOM-presence-only suite once passed while the entire stage was 0px tall.
